title: Curvefield — Elliptic-Curve Cryptography Lab
description: An interactive ECC lab: the geometric group law, finite-field curves, a from-scratch secp256k1 cryptosystem (ECDSA + BIP-340 Schnorr + MuSig2), Curve25519, and a hand-written BLS12-381 pairing — now standards-grade and the substrate for a from-scratch Groth16 zk-SNARK. RFC 9380 hash-to-curve (expand_message_xmd, Simplified SWU with the 11-/3-isogenies, cofactor clearing) reproduces the RFC's own G1/G2 test vectors; ZCash/Ethereum point serialization, IRTF BLS signatures with HKDF KeyGen (matching the EIP-2333 vector) and proof-of-possession, and an optimized Hayashida–Aranha final exponentiation round out a production-shaped pairing stack. The Groth16 lab proves knowledge of a secret (x³+x+5=out) in three group elements, verified by one pairing equation, and a from-scratch PLONK lab proves the *same* statement with a universal setup — selector-gate arithmetization, a copy-constraint permutation argument, a grand-product polynomial, and a transparent KZG-batched verifier — so the two SNARKs sit side by side. From-scratch BULLETPROOFS add a Fiat–Shamir inner-product argument that folds the witness in half each round, giving aggregated logarithmic range proofs (a 64-bit amount in ~16 group elements, ~20× smaller than the linear form) checked by both a transparent and an optimized single-multiexp verifier, plus a full confidential transaction — homomorphic kernel-excess balance over an aggregated range proof, the Monero/Mimblewimble structure, with an inflation attack shown to break it. Plus Schnorr adaptor signatures and atomic swaps, BIP-32 HD wallets, Shamir/Feldman secret sharing, FROST threshold signatures, zero-knowledge Σ-protocols (Pedersen, Chaum–Pedersen, range proofs), KZG polynomial commitments, and discrete-log + invalid-curve attacks — all computed live in the browser, pinned to published test vectors, with zero crypto dependencies.
